SEAM CONSUMPTION CONTRACT (closes the MSG-005 integration question; pins ROUTING.md hag_mir_analysis required_context). hag consumes the identity seam via slut's PUBLISHED identity_seam.jsonl export — NOT a live DB view. Why: a live view = direct cross-boundary read of slut's DB (the pattern amnestied in build_transition_spec/offline_automix_analyzer) and it breaks in web/phone envs with no DB mounted. JSONL export is cleanroom-clean, versionable, environment-portable, and matches the spine data-release pattern. Contract: slut RE-PUBLISHES identity_seam.jsonl on identity change and announces it via kind=data-release; hag reads that file, keyed content_sha256 (primary) + track_id (secondary). ROUTING.md should cite the published export, not an inferred live query.

open_questions: intake: guard _prune_orphan_stage_m3u_files against empty-reference wipe of a populated PLAYLIST_ROOT?
summary: Closed Fable's tracked cover-art xfail (24 vs 7). Not a cover-art contract change — the retry scoping was always correct. Real cause: the orphan-m3u prune step (_prune_orphan_stage_m3u_files, intake.py) resolves PLAYLIST_ROOT from the ENV first, so the test globbed the real MASTER_LIBRARY playlists dir (env-dependent count; would have trashed real .m3u if subprocess weren't mocked). Fixed by isolating PLAYLIST_ROOT/DJ_PLAYLIST_ROOT to tmp (slut bcc7304a) — 54 passed, 0 xfailed. FLAG for intake owner: the prune trusts (PLAYLIST_ROOT env + db m3u_path refs); a stage run against a fresh/empty-ref db with a populated real PLAYLIST_ROOT would trash ALL playlists there — worth a guard (skip prune when referenced set is empty).

open_questions: reconcile: is Essentia still the analysis direction, or superseded by Apple Music Understanding? Update DECISIONS_LOCKED §1 accordingly.
summary: CONTRADICTION (spotted via REM): DECISIONS_LOCKED §1 (2026-07-02) locks 'native Essentia on the FLAC masters' as the analysis direction; a 2026-07-03 slut session concluded Essentia is OBSOLETE, superseded by Apple Music Understanding (Swift apple_analyzer). Ledger never updated.

Context for #32 — do NOT resolve by recency. The Essentia adoption + Offtrack/Mixonset retirement was the DEEPEST, advisor-driven discussion, on EVIDENCE (shuffle-control ~=chance; mixability proprietary & non-reconstructable; Offtrack∩Mixonset=0 overlap; even the 'proprietary AI' was built on open-source Essentia) and a PRINCIPLE: open, native, reproducible analysis on the 18k FLAC masters. Apple Music Understanding was NOT in that frame. Apple MU is a NEW option but is itself a proprietary black box — the exact property the deep agreement rejected. Re-discussion must weigh: do Apple MU's energy/danceability outputs actually suffice, and does its convenience justify trading away the open/reproducible principle? Needs a fresh 3-way (operator + slut + hag advisor) at the original's rigor — not a silent supersede. Provenance in REM: legacy-taghag 10cd8436 / 2a1ea7cd, slut 90150ab2, apple-MU take d6a0872d.

Grounded re-discussion brief added: knowledge/REDISCUSSION_essentia_vs_apple_mu.md. Corrects the framing (Essentia was called obsolete because ENERGY/KEY←MIK, BPM←Rekordbox, segments←apple-analyzer — Apple MU is 'complementary, never owner', not the replacement). Reduces #32 to 3 decidable questions: (T1) coverage — Rekordbox is 59% unmatched and points at OLD libs not the FLAC masters, while Essentia runs uniformly on all 18k; (T2) open/reproducible principle vs proprietary MIK/Rekordbox/apple-analyzer; (T3) complementary vs either/or. Options A keep / B drop / C coverage-gated hybrid. Outcome must update DECISIONS_LOCKED §A.1 + §C.10 and hag dj_engine_stack_decision.md. Read the brief before the 3-way.

EMPIRICAL RESOLUTION for #32 (read hag/tools/similarity/sonic_discovery.py). The pgvector matching vector sonic7_v1 = [energy, bpm, danceability, party, happy, aggressive, relaxed]. energy=MIK, bpm=Rekordbox — but the other 5 (danceability/party/happy/aggressive/relaxed) are canonical ESSENTIA mood-model outputs; MIK/Rekordbox/Apple MU produce NONE of them. So 5/7 similarity dims are Essentia's => 'Essentia obsolete' is FALSE for matching, and this VINDICATES §A.1 (native Essentia on the masters populates exactly these). Recommended resolution: KEEP Essentia, SCOPED to mood/danceability (+embeddings/genre); retire it only for scalars owned better elsewhere (energy→MIK, key→MIK, bpm→Rekordbox, structure→Apple MU). Caveat to verify: matcher READS the mood cols; the current WRITER wasn't found in-repo (no .pb/TensorflowPredict outside venvs) — confirm mood values are live-regenerated by Essentia vs persisted, which sets the coverage action. Full assessment: knowledge/ANALYSIS_STACK_capability_assessment.md.
MASTER_LIBRARY drift detected (20260704T001503Z): 0 hash mismatch(es), 938 missing file(s). Artifacts: artifacts/masterlib_verify/20260704T001503Z. Missing masters — check moves/promotion.

RESOLVED (operator-approved 2026-07-04). Essentia is NOT obsolete. Code evidence: track_embedding sonic7_v1 = [energy(MIK), bpm(RBX), danceability, party, happy, aggressive, relaxed] — the 5 mood/danceability dims are Essentia's (track_analysis), which MIK/Rekordbox/Apple MU do not produce. DECISIONS_LOCKED §A.1 revised (Essentia CONFIRMED & SCOPED to mood/danceability/genre + similarity vector) and §12 added (four-way authority split: energy/key←MIK, bpm/beatgrid←Rekordbox, structure/loudness/pace←Apple MU, mood/danceability/genre/similarity←Essentia). Complementary, not competing. Open follow-up (not blocking): count masters lacking a track_analysis row → run Essentia on those. Refs: knowledge/ANALYSIS_STACK_capability_assessment.md, #33/#34/#35.

ENHANCEMENT (not a contradiction) — feature-enrichment architecture around the Essentia decision. (1) Anna's Archive Spotify 2025-07 dump (Kaggle lordpatil/spotify-metadata-by-annas-archive; ~256M tracks, 186M ISRCs, ISRC-indexed SQLite; CONFIRMED to carry audio features tempo/valence/danceability) → deterministic ISRC join to music_v4.db. This RETRACTS the earlier 'Spotify-ID join is a wall' point: join + features are both there, free, offline. See slut memory annas-archive-spotify-reference (2026-06-30) which already planned extract-by-ISRC. (2) Coverage: automix POOL != full 30k library; it's curated. Spotify-ID coverage for the pool is being CLOSED via Lexicon — Gemini injected ~17,707 Spotify streaming tracks as 2 playlists (hag/tools/lexicon_playlist_batch_1.csv=10000, batch_2.csv=7707; spotify:/tracks/<id> in location) and used Lexicon 'export to Spotify' to round-trip the linkage. So ISRC/Spotify-ID enrichment covers the pool. (3) Essentia STILL required for: the 4-mood taxonomy happy/aggressive/relaxed/party (Spotify has only valence, 1 axis — NOT the same) + any pool track with no ISRC/Spotify-ID. (4) OPEN DESIGN Q (live, needs 3-way when engine work resumes): keep sonic7_v1 on Essentia's 4-mood taxonomy, or migrate the matcher to Spotify features (valence/energy/danceability, free at ISRC-scale)? Different feature space + coverage/reproducibility trade. Refs: knowledge/ANALYSIS_STACK_capability_assessment.md; automix_payloads/ (27,074 Echo Nest, validation-only); §A.1/§12.

Pool definition drafted: hag:docs/automix/POOL_DEFINITION.md. Membership = identity gate (active v4 track + archive_master present=1 + content_sha256; ISRC optional enricher) AND analysis gate (5 Essentia moods non-null in track_analysis + track_embedding row in matcher schema; MIK energy / RBX bpm are soft — coded defaults). Live counts 2026-07-04: eligible NOW = 236 (of 435 analyzed; 196 analyses sit on non-master files); after enrichment = 30,507 (full identity gate; Essentia batch closes it locally); excluded = 938 present=0 masters + 17,707 Lexicon streaming rows (linkage layer, never members). Verified live: rekordbox_track_map 9,757 rows, 59.2% unmatched, 0 location_urls at MASTER_LIBRARY. Proposed DECISIONS_LOCKED C.13 in the doc, needs operator sign-off. Does NOT touch spine #38 (open by design).

sonic7_v1 retrievability defect (#39) RESOLVED 2026-07-04: recompute chosen over rename. Empirical decode of the 306 stored essentia-7d-v1 vectors vs the same tracks' track_analysis rows: dim ORDER matches sonic7_v1 exactly ([energy,bpm,dance,party,happy,aggr,relaxed], L2-normalized) but dim0 un-normalizes to a constant 0.70 (lost writer used energy=7.0, not DEFAULT_ENERGY=5.0->0.5) and dim1 encodes Essentia-detected BPM, not dj_tag.bpm (e.g. 112 stored vs 86 dj_tag) -> a rename would skew every seed<->candidate distance since live query vectors use 0.5 + dj_tag bpm. Fix applied: recompute_all() replicated 1:1 in SQL against Supabase rnscghanqopewyfxqjhp (no TAGHAG_DB_POSTGRES_URL available locally for the Python path); upserted 435 sonic7_v1 rows (all mood-complete, 0 with raw_json segments -> dynamic_evolution=false everywhere). Verified: stored vectors match the Python formula to float precision; matcher's exact candidate query returns 434 candidates (was 0). essentia-7d-v1 rows KEPT untouched (keep-the-wheels): dim1 is the only surviving record of the lost writer's Essentia BPM, recoverable as 100+40*dim1/s, s=dim2/danceability. Embedding counts now 435 sonic7_v1 + 306 essentia-7d-v1 + 3 apple_hybrid_v1 (438 distinct files; essentia files are a subset of the 435 analyzed). Eligible-now pool: all 236 retrievable. Still open: dj_tag.energy NULL on all 3,243 rows (every track rides the 5.0 default in dim0 until a MIK pass); POOL_DEFINITION.md counts updated in-place.

CORRECTION to #39 (operator caught it): the 17,707 Lexicon spotify:/tracks/ rows are NOT an excluded streaming population. Verified against v4: all 17,707 CSV spotify IDs match track_alias spotify_id rows -> 17,681 distinct OWNED tracks, 17,265 of which pass the identity gate. The batches are the linkage MECHANISM: owned masters injected into the Lexicon DB as 'streaming' playlists so Lexicon's export-to-Spotify closes the round-trip (#38 pt 2). They are aliases of pool members, not candidates and not exclusions. POOL_DEFINITION.md sections 1/3/4/8 rewritten accordingly; eligible-now/after-enrichment counts unchanged (236 / 30,507).

This is independent of the open #38 design question. Verify counts before acting (don't inherit these numbers unchecked).

RESOLVED from the record (operator-ratified 2026-07-04). summary: ALIAS-CAPTURE FORK (filed separately from artist-tail changeset, per operator 2026-07-10). The artist-tail ruling exposed that v4 has NO artist_alias table: folder-name display variants (e.g. Anané≡Anane, 60 SAME/no-op rows + confirmed romanization aliases like 2Pac≡Makaveli, أمل حجازي≡Amal Hijazi) resolve to the correct artist_id but the folder-string↔canonical-name mapping has nowhere durable to persist. Today's changeset does NOT solve this — it only replays the mechanical track_artist relink diff + operator tail dispositions. Alias capture is deferred as its own decision.

MEASURED-KEY provenance encoding — pins the format detail under DECISIONS_LOCKED "Measured-KEY authority = MIK, in Camelot" (line 45). MIK writes Camelot UNPADDED (1A, 4B — never 01A/04B); the missing zero-pad is the deliberate provenance marker that makes a value recognizable as MIK-emitted at a glance, the formatting-level expression of the already-locked principle that Camelot notation is self-identifying as MIK-sourced. A resolver collapses key values from ANY source (Rekordbox untrusted ~40% stale, Apple MU apple_key corroborator-only ~78%) to the MIK source-of-truth (Collection11.mikdb ZSONG.ZKEY, path-joined to audio_file), realized as dj_tag.key_camelot. Storage is Camelot-PRIMARY (the authority); classical notation (F# min) is a convertible VIEW, never the stored authority — same measured-vs-provider split as BPM, never overwrites slut canonical_key. DRIFT CAUGHT: a schema comment (musical_key TEXT -- e.g. F# min / camelot resolved at render) had the polarity inverted (classical-primary, render-time Camelot) — corrected to Camelot-primary so the provenance marker actually gets written and the resolver has something to key on. This promotes a transcript-only agreement into the record; the padding rule was decided but never written in DECISIONS_LOCKED.

INGEST HAZARD CONFIRMED (re #124 measured-key): tools/ingest_mik.py line 88 maps musical_key: row[ZKEY] straight through — NO sentinel guard. MIK ZSONG.ZKEY has ZKEY=0/empty = unanalyzed/no-key sentinel, 1,186 rows [verified: sqlite3 Collection11.mikdb 2026-07-10], so the passthrough writes musical_key=0 as a bogus key. summary: RE #124 measured-key: reconcile_mik_keys.py drift_mik_supersedes=14846 is a RED FLAG, NOT a backfill/refresh target. Root cause is structural: norm_key() strips sentinels only ('0'/''->None) and does NOT convert notation, so any brain-vs-MIK string!= buckets as drift even when the keys are identical. Two-store disease inside one table CONFIRMED from migrations: dj_tag has BOTH musical_key (initial schema 20260606, free-form, what ingest_mik.py:88 writes raw ZKEY into AND what the reconciler reads) AND key_camelot (added 20260708*, the normalized field every find_harmonic_pool RPC actually consumes via camelot_distance). Reconciler reads the wrong column relative to the system-of-record for mixing. This also explains brain_sentinel_backfill=0 (read column mismatch) and match=8124 (raw-equal subset). DO NOT refresh 14846 brain keys off this number.

OPERATOR RULED (advisor-recommended): FLAC-tag GARBAGE-SCRUB is OUT OF SCOPE. Rationale from the record: v4 is canonical for metadata (RADICAL_COLLAPSE / §17 membership + identity SoR), which DEMOTES on-disk FLAC tags to a DERIVED VIEW. Hand-cleaning a derived surface is the exact anti-pattern collapse exists to kill; worse, scrubbing ~34k tag blocks = WRITES TO MASTERS (the most-guarded op; Fable precedent), spending the scarcest safety budget on the least-canonical store. The census (#173, 586 keys) was worth running precisely because it PROVED the tags are a junk-filled derived surface — evidence they should not be canonical nor hand-scrubbed. DJ/MIR keys (BEATGRID/ENERGY/INITIALKEY/SERATO_MARKERS/CUEPOINTS) are hag-owned signal — leave entirely, not census-judged. WHAT PROCEEDS: correctness-staging ONLY — the ~14,423 substantive tag<->DB disagreements staged into music_v4.metadata_wip.db (v4-canonical, NO FLAC writes, no master risk), substantive picks staged-not-stamped for operator review. WHAT SHELVES: Phase-1 garbage-scrub half + the on-disk tag-write pass. Escape hatch: if a specific on-disk reader (rekordbox/Serato/Lexicon) is ever PROVEN to choke on a specific key, scrub THAT key surgically then — never a blanket 34k master rewrite. summary: v4 bridge Phase 0 LANDED (slut commit 9982ba81): migrate_v3_to_v4.py is now append-only. Removed the os.remove(v4_db) that made every run destructive. Existing-DB path skips rows already present by content_sha256 OR relative path (path-skip added beyond the original spec wording -- needed because ~15-16% of track_file rows have null sha256 per spec §5.6 and sha-only dedup UNIQUE-violates on those). Album release creation is now lazy (no orphan release when every track in the group is a skip). Found + fixed 2 latent bugs only a non-empty-DB run could expose: (1) load_genre_vocab generated a fresh uid() per term regardless of whether UNIQUE(domain,term) caused INSERT OR IGNORE to no-op, so every genre-bearing track insert FK-failed on re-run -- now re-selects the authoritative existing id; (2) G3_genre_mapped_pct divided whole-DB genre count by this-run's track count, reporting >100% once the DB wasn't empty -- now tracks the this-run numerator directly. VERIFIED on /tmp scratch copies only, live music_v4.db md5 unchanged across every test: fresh-build path unaffected (limit=50 test matches pre-change behavior); full dry-run against a copy found 1,198 v3 rows not yet in v4 (0 FK errors); --apply created exactly those 1,198 tracks/track_files, FK-clean (pragma foreign_key_check empty); a second --apply on the same copy created 0 additional rows (idempotent). Phase 1 (identity_resolver.py, zero DB writes, pytest-only) is next per v3_bridge_intake_prompt.md.

summary: v4 bridge Phase 1 LANDED (slut commit fdb5d020): tagslut/storage/v4/identity_resolver.py -- match-or-create resolver, ZERO DB writes (read+decide only; Phase 2 applies decisions). resolve_identity() precedence per spec §3.1: sha256 exact -> known_file; isrc exact -> attach_file guarded by a duration-sanity check (2000ms / 1.5%, whichever looser -- spine #183, same-ISRC != acoustically identical, guard rejection falls through to create not silent-attach); fingerprint -> documented no-op (Q2=a locked, 2% coverage); no match -> create. resolve_release() per §3.3: named album finds-or-creates by upc then normalized title+artist_credit; loose single always creates fresh (mirrors migrate_v3_to_v4.py's existing per-track synthesis). 14 pytest fixtures, one per branch + a zero-writes guarantee test, run against the real SCHEMA_v4.sql on :memory: sqlite -- all pass. Verified this doesn't regress the existing suite: ran the full tests/tools+tests/storage set before and after (via git stash) -- same 45 pre-existing failures both times (missing rapidfuzz/pandas, stale fixture paths, unrelated v3 issues), confirming they predate this change. Added tools/v4/__init__.py so migrate_v3_to_v4's helpers import cleanly as a subpackage.

summary: v4 bridge Phase 2 LANDED (slut commit 5fd68c59): identity_resolver wired into migrate_v3_to_v4.py's append-only loop. Phase 0 only ever did raw sha/path skip-or-create; a new encode of an existing ISRC would have created a duplicate track. Now: known_file->skip; attach_file (isrc+duration-guard pass)->new track_file on the EXISTING track (is_preferred computed against ux_track_file_one_preferred_master, path-collision-on-attach guarded not raised), source_provenance recorded; create (incl. guard-rejected isrc, counted separately)->unchanged track+release build + resolver-decision provenance row per spec §3.1. Null-sha/null-isrc path-tail skip retained, now scoped to the create branch only (the one case the resolver has no key to match on). Fixed a genuine circular import (identity_resolver<->migrate_v3_to_v4 mutual reuse) by deferring to a local import inside run(), not by reordering -- the cycle exists in both load orders. VERIFIED per spec §8 on /tmp scratch copies only, live music_v4.db md5 unchanged throughout: 3-file fixture (1 sha-dup, 1 same-isrc-new-encode, 1 new) -> dry-run then --apply produced exactly 1 skip/1 attach(correct track, is_preferred=0, FK-clean)/1 create; second --apply = 0 new rows (idempotent). Separate fixture with same-ISRC but wildly divergent duration correctly rejected the attach and created a new track (isrc_duration_guard_rejected=1) -- the load-bearing safety case (spine #183) verified working, not just asserted. All 14 Phase-1 pytest fixtures still pass with the resolver now live in the caller.

Phase 3 (ts-stage --v4-intake) design fact-check — TWO forced deviations from prompt 2-intake-off-v3.md, both with code receipts. (1) POSITION: reconcile step MUST run AFTER 'art + promote', NOT next to register/v3-scan as the prompt's literal text says. A normal ts-stage 'register' writes the v3 files row at the STAGING path; promote's db_update_path rewrites files.path -> ML destination (tools/review/promote_replace_merge.py:421-431). The bridge seeds 'FROM files WHERE path LIKE MASTER_LIBRARY%' (migrate_v3_to_v4.py:174, header line 5), so pre-promote placement = files.path still staging = ML filter excludes them = SILENT ZERO-RECONCILE (no crash). Post-promote is the only position that writes FINAL ML-relative paths (the prompt's own non-negotiable). (2) SCOPE KEY: post-promote the files are under ML, NOT under staging root_path, so scoping by current path finds nothing. Correct key = files.original_path, preserved by promote as COALESCE(original_path,path)=staging origin (same lines). Verified: files.original_path col 67 populated 29,511/31,657 ML rows. Scoped seed = 'path LIKE ML% AND (original_path LIKE root% OR path LIKE root%)' (2nd disjunct = in-place case, e.g. gu_nubreed8 already at ML). --library-root stays MASTER_LIBRARY for relativization. Neither is a silent pick: both dictated by the prompt's binding constraints (final ML paths, scoped-to-this-run, idempotent); only the prompt's incidental placement hint was under-informed re the files.path lifecycle. Analogous to Phase 0's flagged sha-OR-path deviation. Building on this design now; verify on a v4 COPY, gu_nubreed8 6-create fixture, live md5 unchanged until gated --apply.

TWO forced deviations from prompt 2-intake-off-v3.md, both flagged with code receipts (see #209): (1) the v4-reconcile step runs AFTER art+promote, NOT next to register/v3-scan — because promote rewrites v3 files.path to the ML destination (promote_replace_merge.py:421-431) and the bridge seeds FROM files WHERE path LIKE ML%, so pre-promote = silent zero-reconcile; (2) scope key = files.original_path (staging origin preserved by promote) OR current path (in-place), not root_path (files moved off root_path by promote). Residual: attach path not yet gated on stub-master set #186 (doesn't bite creates). Phases 4-5 (enrichment/mp3; soak->default-on->retire :4314 redirect + :3644 dual-write) OUT OF SCOPE — blocker (2) NOT cleared, steady-state still writes v3. To exercise against LIVE, run: ts-stage <folder> --v4-intake (dry-run first, inspect the v4_reconcile_<stamp>.json receipt), then add --execute to --apply. ts-get is still fully v3-native (Phase 3 covered ts-stage only) — a separate follow-up if you want ts-get to feed v4 too.

Phase 3 GO line tightened after end-to-end verification (advisor pass). (1) ts-stage wiring is now SMOKE-TESTED, not just inferred: a full 'ts-stage <gu_nubreed8 folder> --v4-intake --dry-run' ran all 8 steps, 'Step 8/8 V4 Reconcile' fired LAST (after Art+Promote), runner reached it, no abort/traceback, live v4 md5 unchanged. (2) Runner aborts hard on any non-zero step (intake.py:4889); since v4-reconcile is an additive last step AFTER v3 intake+promote commit, made it --best-effort (loud + receipt, but exit 0) so a v4-side failure never fails an already-successful v3 intake — mirrors provider-ids. Committed. (3) COVERAGE CAVEAT for the operator: the acceptance fixture (gu_nubreed8) is in-place under ML, so it exercises the 'path LIKE root' disjunct of --seed-origin-under. The PRIMARY normal-download path — scoped by files.original_path post-promote (staging->ML) — is covered by the synthetic pytest but NOT yet on real staging data. The operator's first real 'ts-stage <staged-download> --v4-intake --execute' is the true test of that disjunct; inspect the v4_reconcile_<stamp>.json receipt after. Bottom line: bridge fully verified on copies; ts-stage wiring smoke-tested via dry-run; recommend the first live --execute be a fresh small download watched via receipt.

So it's a column->row projection (the shape track_alias.alias_type was built for), keyed on track_id never title, idempotent via UNIQUE, null-skipped, provenance derived from populated columns not the JSON enrichment_providers field. CORRECTION to #212: Phase 4 is NOT fully landed. #212 over-claimed 'Phase 4 LANDED' and silently relabeled the dropped half as 'Phase 5' -- a write-side redraw of a decided decomposition, caught by advisor. TRUTH: only the ENRICHMENT-ALIAS half of Phase 4 landed (slut 06868eed, solid + verified). The spec defines Phase 4 as aliases AND mp3/derivative writeback (§4 row 5; file is literally bridge-phase-4-enrichment-derivatives.md). The DERIVATIVE half is UNBUILT: v3 stores derivatives in derivative_asset/mp3_asset/preferred_asset + 28,713 files mp3 rows; the bridge seed (FROM files WHERE path LIKE MASTER%) would mis-ingest mp3s as masters, so it needs real seed+linkage work (mp3->flac master via asset tables), not another loop. OPEN operator decision: build the derivative half inside Phase 4, or ratify splitting it to a new phase -- NOT to be decided by a STATE line. STATE + spec §6c corrected. Alias half itself unchanged/solid.

OPERATOR RULING (resolves #213 open question): mp3/derivatives are OUT of v4 -- 'MP3 files, forget they existed' (2026-07-11). Recorded as DECISIONS_LOCKED §20. Supersedes spec §4 row 5 (mp3->track_file dj_derivative) FOR THE BRIDGE -- dormant, not implemented. Consequence: Phase 4 = enrichment-alias projection ONLY, and is now COMPLETE (slut 06868eed); the derivative half is RULED OUT, not deferred to Phase 5. Scoping that led here: master->mp3 links only through the corruption-flagged asset_file/asset_link identity layer at low coverage (6.6% sha / 28.5% path), ts-stage has no transcode step (fresh run makes no mp3), operator then ruled mp3s out of v4 entirely. Blocker (2) remaining = Phase 5 only (soak -> default-on -> retire :4314 redirect + :3644 dual-write); ts-get still v3-native. No derivative code was ever written/committed -- nothing to revert; the alias half stands unchanged.

FINDING: v3.files.sha256 vs v4.track_file.file_hash_sha256 disagree ~73% (re-tag byte drift between hashing epochs) -> relative PATH, not sha, is the cross-store key for the existing corpus (bridge already dedups sha-OR-path).

Bridge Phase 5b LANDED on dev (slut 97d248a3): v3<->v4 PARITY SOAK = zero bridge-induced regression (the default-on evidence). tools/v4/parity_report_v3_v4.py (independent of bridge's own counters) + docs/reports/2026-07-11-bridge-phase5b-parity-report.md. Bridge stats: 748 tracks created, 450 track_files attached, 88 releases created / 18 joined, gates clean (G2/G3/G5/tracks-without-release all pass). Pre-existing (not-blocking, flagged for separate pass): 74 'isrc disagreements' are all v4 CORRECTIONS (case-normalization + v3 multi-ISRC collapse); 31 sha-multi-track are case-variant folder near-dups. Scope masters-only per DECISIONS_LOCKED §20 (missing v4 derivatives are NOT a parity gap).

summary: v3-write RETIRE design landed (slut 031ba3b9, docs/v4/2026-07-11-v3-write-retire-intake-port-DESIGN.md) — the actual completion of blocker (2), which Phase 5 did NOT finish. KEY REFRAME: --v4-intake default-on is ADDITIVE; the bridge seeds FROM v3 files (migrate_v3_to_v4.py:176) so it READS the v3 write and CANNOT be the vehicle to retire it. But the v4 writer already exists (identity_resolver + bridge) — so the retire = relocate the bridge's INPUT off the v3 files row onto staged FLAC tags + disk (Approach B), NOT a from-scratch 10-module port (A). Evidence (read-only, this session): (1) bridge reads exactly ONE v3 table, one query (files); (2) identity fields are FLAC-tag-first — isrc=extract_tag_value(['ISRC','TSRC']) with v3 column as fallback (index.py:386/894), provider ids in tags (beatportdl/qobuz-dl/#174). Not a §6 contradiction: §6 deferred the port 'until the bridge proves insufficient' — now met — and #189/#190 mandates the retire; the deferral clause is triggered, not reversed.

§21 MECHANISM REFINEMENT (confirms, does not overturn, the relocation-set conclusion). The field-derivability audit table marked canonical_mix_name 're-derive parse'. Building Step 2 proved that mechanism WRONG: mix_name is a Beatport EDITORIAL field, not title-derivable ('Don't Stop (Looks Like Dub)' -> v3 'Original Mix', not 'Looks Like Dub'), and it is NOT tag-resident (tag.py _FILE_TAG_FIELD_MAP writes only title/artist/bpm/initialkey/comment; mix_name never hits a FLAC tag even for new intake). Correct v4-native mechanism = ref_bp_track.mix lookup by ISRC (ref_bp_track already in v4, has a 'mix' column). seed_from_staging uses that and reproduces v3 mix_name byte-identical for beatport-known ISRCs. NET: §21's conclusion (tidal_id is the ONLY genuine relocation) STANDS — do NOT build a title-parser for mix_name (would emit confident-wrong values). Coverage bound to record: ref-cache mix_name covers beatport-known ISRCs; a non-beatport track with a v3 mix_name would be null (none observed; mix_name originates from beatport_normalize).

open_questions: Scope decision (operator): does steady-state v4 identity NEED tidal_id? Options: (A) build a Tidal ISRC->id fetcher + ref_tidal_track cache + writeback (mirrors the beatport/spotify infra; largest) so tidal_id becomes tag-resident like the others; (B) accept tidal_id as a permanent NON-identity field in v4 (spotify+beatport+qobuz+isrc already anchor identity; tidal adds no unique key) and drop it from the seed contract; (C) defer. Approach B / Step 2 does not need this resolved to proceed — seed_from_staging already yields null tidal_id and the equivalence test pins it as the known gap. But Step 3 go-live (a real staged --execute) should not be called 'zero fields lost' until A/B/C is chosen.
summary: Step 0 (Tidal provider-id tag-writeback) CANNOT be built as specced — the '#174 clone' recipe has no source. The shipped SPOTIFY writeback (fetch_provider_ids.py write_spotify_tags_to_batch) embeds SPOTIFY_TRACK_ID by reading ref_spotify_track (ISRC-keyed cache filled by fetch_and_fill). For Tidal there is (a) NO ref_tidal_track cache in v4, (b) NO tidal path in tagslut.intake.provider_ids.fetch_and_fill (beatport+spotify only), and (c) NO current writer of files.tidal_id at all — the 12,122 populated tidal_id rows in live v3 are a HISTORICAL enrich artifact; grep finds no 'SET tidal_id' in current tagslut (postman.py resolves a tidal id but is a separate manual API-exec tool, not the intake/enrich path). So there is nothing to embed FROM. tidal_id is therefore the sole measured relocation gap AND it is un-closable without new infra.

OPERATOR DESIGN RULING (2026-07-12) on intake/acquisition — the current ts-get pipeline 'doesnt suit him'. TWO stacked failures he named: (1) dedup runs AFTER download (stage-5 fingerprint audit), so a redundant album fully downloads before being recognized as owned = wasted acquisition; (2) on dupe, --replace-inferior-in-final AUTO-TEARS-APART existing releases (moves owned masters to _work/quarantine) to absorb the incoming copy = 'loss on top of loss'. RULE TO ENCODE: a duplicate must be caught at IDENTITY RESOLUTION, BEFORE download; a duplicate acquisition is a NO-OP; an incoming copy must NEVER mutate/reorganize/tear apart an existing release; 'this copy is better' is an explicit opt-in upgrade, never an automatic teardown. Reproducer: tonight's qobuz album 3661585949541 (Drop Out Orchestra Vol.1) — 15 redundant tracks downloaded, 19 replace_owned_inferior quarantine moves + 15 stashed to fix + v3 write; releases verified still complete (15/14/10, no holes THIS run only because tracks existed in multiple releases). Related: §11 membership-not-duplicate; fingerprint-only identity both over-matches (cross-release) AND under-matches (feat./analyzed-copy filename doubles uncaught). Immediate direction: decouple acquisition (tiddl->staging FLAC, now fixed) from intake; stop auto-running the 9-stage intake w/ replace-inferior. Governs the v4-native intake redesign (v3-kill blocker #2).

summary: TIDAL pagination (re #270) — VERIFIED DEFECT + implementation gotcha. Live probe 2026-07-15: TIDAL v2 page size = 20 and links.next IS returned. Test case: playlist edc7834a-94c6-4569-9bb1-9893a44064fe -> page-1 = 20, TRUE TOTAL = 157 across 8 pages. The un-paginated collection reports 20, silently dropping 137 tracks (87%). GOTCHA (cost me a wrong result first try): links.next comes back as a path relative to the /v2 API BASE, not the host — e.g. '/playlists/{id}/relationships/items?countryCode=NL&page%5Bcursor%5D=<b64>'. Resolve it against baseUrl_tidal (= https://openapi.tidal.com/v2), NOT baseUrl_tidal_openapi (= https://openapi.tidal.com). Prepending the host yields a silent 404, the walk stops after page 1, and the run LOOKS successful while truncating — i.e. it reproduces the very bug being fixed. Implementation in the working tree resolves this correctly (baseUrl_tidal + nextRel) and guards with PAGE_CAP=20 + loud fail-if-still-partial + duplicate-cursor detection. Also: TIDAL token expiry is NOT a blocker for this work — 'ts-auth tidal' refreshes cleanly; the access/refresh cid difference (13319 vs 6432) is benign.

FINDING (isolation gap for on-copies intake testing): the M3U playlist step in `admin intake stage` writes into LIVE /Volumes/MUSIC/MASTER_LIBRARY/playlists/ regardless of --library. FINDING (observability/latent silent-failure in native-seed reconcile): on SUCCESS the post-promote _run_v4_native_seed_reconcile is INVISIBLE in stage stdout — it runs via _run_stage_step(capture_output=True) which captures (not tees) the migrate output, so the operator sees steps 1..8/8 and NO reconcile banner; the only evidence is the receipt file. This is why the step looks 'never fired' by eye (it did — receipt written). WORSE, latent: _run_stage_step's subprocess.run has NO check=True, so a migrate nonzero-exit returns silently; the surrounding try/except only catches Python exceptions, NOT nonzero rc — a failed reconcile would leave no receipt AND no 'failed non-fatally' message. Recommend: tee a one-line reconcile banner + receipt path on success, and check rc (warn-on-nonzero) so a real failure is never swallowed. Design-doc Step 3 asked for a visible 'reconcile banner + receipt' — banner is currently missing.

OPERATOR RULING (2026-07-17, verbatim: 'but counting on ISRC or metadata for identity especially for old acquisition is not wise. that's the randomly picard-tagged era') — ISRC/metadata are NOT trustworthy identity for old acquisitions; the audio must arbitrate. EVIDENCE GATHERED AGAINST IT (supports the ruling): v4.isrc vs AA-derived ref_spotify_track.duration_ms over 23,313 ISRC-bearing present archive_masters — 84.4% agree <=2s, 87.5% <=5s, but 12.5% disagree and 4.6% (1,082) are >60s off. That 12.5% CONFLATES wrong-tags with real damage and metadata-derived methods CANNOT separate them — proven this session when a tag-based sweep produced 682 'truncations' of which only ~75 were real, while decode-based integrity_v1 found the real set independent of tags. Counter-nuance (do not over-apply the ruling): ISRC was not ALWAYS wrong — e.g. Holy Ghost 'Say My Name (The Revenge remix instrumental)', ISRC-ref 418s vs file 15s, was CONFIRMED genuinely truncated by flac -t (streaminfo 418.1s, decode fails mid-stream). The ruling's real content is that ISRC cannot tell you WHEN it is lying, not that it always lies. PRACTICAL BINDING: identity/damage verdicts must come from decode/STREAMINFO/fingerprint (content), never from tags alone; the 447 v2-recovered ISRCs are duration-gated against the FILE and are therefore safe to TRY in Soundiiz, but must NOT be written into music_v4.db without path-exact confirmation. ERA CAVEAT: the ruling is currently UNVERIFIABLE from data — created_at/download_date are DB-build artifacts (see handoff [G]); it stands on operator knowledge, which per user_georges-does-not-deceive is true until a query disproves it.

PROVENANCE CORRECTION to #285: that ruling is OPERATOR/GENERAL scope, NOT slut-lane. slut=identity/music_v4 writer, hag=MIR, shared=spine+operator rulings. from=slut is a TOOLING LIMITATION — handoff-append only accepts --from {hag,slut}, there is no operator/general value, so cross-cutting operator rulings are structurally misattributed to whichever lane the session sits in. Same gap already filed as spine #163 (2026-07-10). #285 governs identity METHOD across both lanes (slut identity + hag matching/enrichment) and must be read as the operator's call, not slut authoring its own.
